text: Welcome to Fragmented, a software developer podcast where we talk about building good software and becoming better developers.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: My name is Don Felker.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And I'm Kausha Gopal.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Welcome to the show.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Hey everybody, before we get going, I wanted to let you know about a product that I'm creating called Stoa.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Stoa is a platform for community creators and anyone really who is building a community.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Maybe you have a community that you are passionate about, such as photography, or maybe it's something in regards to cars or motorcycles or woodworking or anything of that nature.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Maybe it's dancing.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Whatever your community is, if you're looking to build it and monetize it, Stoa is a place for you.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: You can have discussions, communicate privately with DMs and private chats, and have various different spaces internally where you can discuss all the different things regarding your community and monetize it as well.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: If this is something that's interesting or you know of someone else who's interested in it, you can go to getstoa.com, plug in your email address, and when we launch, you'll get three months free of the product.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And as well, you can always reach out to me directly if you have questions.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: So again, that is Stoa.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: You can go to getstoa.com for more information.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Thanks.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: All right, welcome back, everybody.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Thanks for joining us on the podcast here.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Kalshik's not with me this time, but though I do have a special guest today that has a story that I really want everybody to hear.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: I think one of the things that's really fundamental about software development is how open it is to anyone who wants to do it.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: If you have the drive, you have the determination, and you want to become a software developer, you can.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: It doesn't really matter your background, doesn't really matter your age.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: For a lot of you that maybe don't even know this, software development is not my first career.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: My first career was actually a cabinetmaker and as well as a motorcycle mechanic.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And I didn't switch careers until my early 20s.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: So I still started kind of early.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: But there are many people that don't switch until their late 20s, their 30s, their 40s, or even later.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And today I have a friend of mine here on the show.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And he is going to help kind of tell the story of how he went from one career, which is not related to software at all.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: into a software development career.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And it's a very interesting story.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: And I kind of want to get some tips and tricks and advice from him for other people that are in a very similar boat and trying to make that transition.
speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: So without further ado, Ephraim, welcome to the show.

text: Thank you, Don.

speaker: SPEAKER_02

text: Sure.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Currently, I'm working at a company called First Form.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: It's health, fitness, supplements.

speaker: SPEAKER_01

speaker: SPEAKER_01

speaker: SPEAKER_01

speaker: SPEAKER_01

speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So I was making very decent money.

speaker: SPEAKER_01

speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: and learn really, really rapidly.
